name: Druva
description: Cloud-native data protection SaaS with AI-driven autonomous recovery, ransomware detection, and backup automation. Protects endpoints, SaaS apps, cloud infra, and data centers. 250+ telecom operators. SOC2, HIPAA, FedRAMP compliant.
oneLiner: Cloud-native data protection SaaS with AI-driven autonomous recovery, ransomware detection, and backup automation.
pricing: Subscription per workload type. Enterprise contract. No published pricing.

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Not an agent builder. Users configure backup policies via web console. AI surfaces as recommendations, anomaly alerts, and recovery suggestions within existing workflow.
strengths: Single control plane across all workloads
weaknesses: Not an agent factory — AI is embedded, not composable
notableFeatures: Automated DR testing without production impact

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Backup validation, recovery testing, detailed logs.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Policy changes on next backup window.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: M365, Google, Salesforce, AWS, Azure, VMware, K8s, NAS, Oracle, SQL.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: No RAG. Backup catalog and metadata queries.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Pause jobs, halt recovery. Ransomware guardrails.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Tamper-proof logging. SOC2, HIPAA, FedRAMP. Legal hold.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Dashboards for health, storage, compliance. SIEM integration.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: Thousands of enterprise customers. Gartner MQ leader.

scoringRationale: Druva scores 70/100. Deployment (17) and Governance (17) strong — carrier-grade SaaS with compliance. Integration (14) broad workloads. Builder (14) decent but not agent-focused. Collaboration (7) minimal. Ecosystem (1) scoring reflects agent-factory-specific evaluation.
targetUser: IT teams automating backup, recovery, and ransomware defense

walkthrough.title: How Druva Works: AI-Driven Data Protection and Recovery

steps.title: Configure Backup Policies
steps.description: Define backup policies via the web console for endpoints, SaaS apps (M365, Google Workspace), cloud workloads (AWS, Azure), and data centers. Set RPO/RTO targets, retention rules, and compliance requirements (HIPAA, FedRAMP, SOC2).

steps.title: AI Detects Anomalies and Threats
steps.description: Druva's AI continuously monitors backup patterns for anomalies indicating ransomware, unusual data deletion, or insider threats. Machine learning models trained on global threat data flag suspicious activity before damage spreads.

steps.title: Autonomous Recovery Orchestration
steps.description: When a threat is detected, Druva identifies the last clean backup, quarantines affected snapshots, and orchestrates recovery. For ransomware, it pinpoints the infection timeline and restores to the most recent clean state.

steps.title: DR Testing with Sandbox Recovery
steps.description: Run disaster recovery drills by restoring to isolated sandbox environments. Validate recovery procedures without affecting production. Generate compliance reports showing DR readiness and audit trails.

steps.title: Predictive Optimization
steps.description: AI analyzes backup patterns to optimize policies: recommend storage tiers, predict capacity needs, identify redundant backups, and suggest policy adjustments. Global deduplication reduces storage costs across the fleet.
